key_facts: Founded 2012 by engineers from Zhejiang University's national power-electronics lab — no parent conglomerate
Listed on Shanghai STAR Market (688032.SH) December 2021
IPO: CNY 5.58B raised at China's highest-ever IPO price at the time
S-Miles Cloud platform: launched 2021 for module-level real-time monitoring
S-Miles Cloud features: parallel-computing cloud, open API, real-time module-level data
Microinverters = module-level power electronics (MLPE) — each module has its own inverter
Key distinction: MLPE provides module-level monitoring data vs string inverter (aggregate only)

funding_history.notes: Highest-ever IPO price in China at the time. Pure engineering startup with no conglomerate backing — funded entirely through business operations and IPO.

business_model_notes: Hardware-enabled monitoring SaaS: microinverter hardware (per-module DC→AC conversion) bundled with S-Miles Cloud monitoring subscription. Every Hoymiles microinverter sold includes S-Miles Cloud activation, creating organic SaaS user base growth proportional to hardware sales. Revenue: hardware margin + recurring cloud service fees. Key advantage over string-inverter OEMs: microinverter generates module-level granular data that string inverters cannot, making the monitoring layer inherently more valuable.
competitive_position: Global microinverter leader; directly competes with Enphase (US, global #1), SolarEdge (US), and domestic players. In China: unique microinverter position in residential rooftop market where string inverters dominate. S-Miles Cloud competes with SOLARMAN and OEM monitoring platforms but differentiated by module-level granularity.
research_notes: Highest confidence (★5). IPO documents publicly filed with STAR Market. Revenue from official annual report. S-Miles Cloud launch and features documented in PRNewswire 2021 press releases. Founded by ZJU engineers with no parent — confirmed by STAR Market IPO prospectus which discloses founding history.
